title: onobrychis cornuta f. pulvillus
intro: Onobrychis cornuta f. pulvillus, also known as a variant of the common name 'beech spurry', is a plant species that belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Onobrychis cornuta f. pulvillus include reducing inflammation, soothing respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to address various health concerns and promote overall well-being.
